desc: Granada lies at the foot of the Sierra Nevada, where Moorish palace architecture, Albaicín lanes, and Spanish tapas culture meet. Visit for the Alhambra, mountain-backed viewpoints, and a layered Andalusian identity shaped by Islamic, Jewish, and Christian history.
hls: Sunset views from Mirador de San Nicolás
Rich fusion of Moorish and Spanish heritage

todo.name: Alhambra timed-entry visit
todo.desc: Reserve early for Nasrid Palaces, Generalife gardens, and Alcazaba fortress views over Granada.

todo.name: Albaicín neighborhood walk
todo.desc: Get lost in whitewashed lanes, cármenes, and small plazas in the old Moorish quarter.

todo.name: Sunset at Mirador de San Nicolás
todo.desc: Watch evening light hit the Alhambra with Sierra Nevada peaks behind it.

todo.name: Sacromonte cave flamenco show
todo.desc: Experience zambra-style performances in hillside cave venues tied to Roma traditions.

todo.name: Granada Cathedral and Royal Chapel
todo.desc: Visit key Catholic monuments linked to the Reconquista and Spain’s Catholic Monarchs.

todo.name: Tapas crawl around Centro
todo.desc: Move between bars where drinks are commonly paired with complimentary local tapas.

wifi.note: Reliable in Albaicín and center stays; hillside properties can see weaker indoor coverage.

prac.fltNote: Granada Airport has limited international routes; many UK visitors fly to Málaga (AGP) then transfer by bus/train (about 2 hours).
prac.visa: Spain is in Schengen; many non-EU travelers (including US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.
alerts: Alhambra tickets often sell out days or weeks ahead in spring and autumn.

sweet: April to June and September to October are best for sightseeing; midsummer afternoons are very hot.
